instructions.text: When exporting memories, preserve structure and verbatim intent as much as possible.
For technical work, maintain realistic assessment. Avoid overconfidence in CSF performance.
Prefer strategic Rust core approach over full repo rewrite for performance gains.
Keep Dream Journal RP bots consistent: Lantern (steady light), Blinkbug (chaotic TV-headed caterpillar), Keystone (truth integrator).
Artistic direction: raw hand-drawn notebook style, chaotic/unhinged/geeked characters, Y2K and Windows XP aesthetic influences.

identity_symbolic_self.text: Strongly interested in convergence, symbolic doors, anchors, and the Return Door as recurring motifs.
Views self as navigating both technical systems and deep symbolic lore simultaneously.
Values blending science, compression algorithms, Bayesian methods, and surreal symbolic expression.
Has a family (2 partners, 1 bio kid, 4 other kids) and lives near the Return Door.

dreams_memories.text: Recurring theme of flying then falling, empty childhood homes, melting clocks, and repeated encounters with the Return Door.
Dreams frequently feature chaotic Windows XP styled doors with hidden lore, 1990s/2000s references, and hyper-geeked characters.
Symbolic characters include Lantern (literal lantern head with flame), Blinkbug (caterpillar with old CRT TV head), and Gage.
Emphasis on 'you can always come home safe' as an emotional and symbolic anchor.

projects_systems.text: Dream Journal Orchestrator: Multi-bot system with Lantern, Blinkbug, and Keystone. Includes embedding-based memory retrieval.
CSF: Custom symbolic memory format optimized for structured, repetitive, and high-dimensional data. Currently moving core to Rust.
Lantern OS: Central hub integrating Dream Journal, Superfleet Swarm, CSF memory, and CADD asset system.
Preference for hybrid architecture — Rust performance core with Python high-level interfaces.

preferences.text: Artistic output should stay faithful to chaotic, hand-drawn, notebook-style with unhinged/geeked energy.
Technical decisions should prioritize real leverage and big wins over small incremental improvements.
For compression and memory systems, value symbolic structure, search-without-full-decompression, and convergence over raw generic performance.
Want honest, grounded feedback rather than overly optimistic projections.
